---
title: "Peer_Review_Shamima"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-20"
---
# Overall read ability
Your code is very readable and laid out very well. It was easy to follow and the steps made logical sense

# Suggestions
In this step "fang_data$SNP_ID <- rownames(fang_data)" I don't think its entirely necessary to rename the rows because in teh merge function it allows you to add this: by.x= "SAMPLE_ID", by.y= "SNP_ID". Now you are telling R which columns you want to merge

For your Maize and teosinte group files you should consider moving the group columns to teh front so you can ensure the code ran properly. It would just act as a double check. You can use this code: 
teosinte_group <- select(Group, everything())
The way it works is by moving the column names lsited before everything() to the front. 


For the data visulaization #2 I used an ifelse statement so I didn't have to type out all the heterozygous genotypes. It would cut down on typing
